title: Subtitle Intelligence
subtitle: Find, score, validate, and install the best subtitle for every title.

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Enable subtitle sources and enter their credentials. Secrets are encrypted at rest.

languages.title: Language Policy
languages.subtitle: Set which subtitle languages each library requires and prefers.

history.title: Downloads & History
history.subtitle: Installed subtitles and the full activity trail.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Module-wide automation, plus how everything fits together.

automation.heading: Automation
automation.intro: These control the hands-off behaviour. They do nothing until at least one provider is enabled and your libraries have a language policy.

interval.label: Automatic library scan
interval.help: How often the background job sweeps every enabled library for items missing their languages. Off means the scan only runs when you trigger it by hand.

autoDownload.label: Auto-download during scans
autoDownload.help: When a scan finds a missing language, download the best acceptable match automatically. Off: the gap is only flagged (a notification + an automation trigger), and you download it yourself.

autoSync.label: Auto-synchronize downloads
autoSync.help: After an automatic download, align the subtitle to the audio with FFsubsync. The original is always kept alongside the synced copy.
autoSync.unavailable: Unavailable — FFsubsync is not installed on this server, so automatic sync can't run (manual offset still works).

defaultLanguages.label: Default languages
defaultLanguages.help: Which languages a library looks for when it has no policy of its own. Comma-separated ISO codes, e.g. "en, es".

pipeline.heading: The pipeline
pipeline.body: For each item: fingerprint it (a movie-hash + technical metadata) → search every enabled provider most-confident-first (exact hash → release name → IMDb/TMDB id → title) → score each candidate 0–100 → validate the file → install a media-server-correct sidecar next to the video, never overwriting an existing one → optionally sync it to the audio.

providers.heading: Providers
providers.body: At least one must be enabled — this is the one mandatory step. OpenSubtitles and SubDL are official APIs (need a key) and give the best matches, including exact hash matches. YIFY, SubtitleCat and Podnapisi are keyless but lower-confidence (YIFY and SubtitleCat scrape public sites, so they're best-effort). Local Repository serves subtitles from a folder you point it at.

scoring.heading: Scoring tiers
scoring.auto: Auto — trusted (e.g. an exact hash match); downloaded and installed automatically.
scoring.download: Download — strong; downloaded, sync-verified, installed if it checks out.
scoring.present: Review — plausible; shown to you, never installed automatically.

languages.heading: Per-library language policy
languages.body: Each library can require and prefer specific languages, demand forced/SDH tracks, set a minimum score, choose preferred providers, and auto-replace. A library with no policy falls back to the Default languages above.

sync.heading: Synchronization
sync.body: Two engines: a pure manual offset (always available) that shifts timings by a fixed amount, and FFsubsync, which listens to the audio and aligns the subtitle automatically. The original is never overwritten.
sync.ffHint: Install ffmpeg + ffsubsync (or rebuild the image with INSTALL_SUBTITLE_SYNC=true) to enable automatic audio sync.

search.title: Subtitle Search
search.subtitle: Search every enabled provider for a media item and install the best match.
search.noResultsHint: Try more languages, or configure another provider.

sync.title: Synchronization
sync.subtitle: Align installed subtitles to the audio. The original is always kept.
sync.ffHint: Install ffmpeg + ffsubsync for automatic audio sync.
sync.emptyHint: Download a subtitle first, then sync it here.

validation.title: Validation
validation.subtitle: Check a subtitle for structural problems before use.
validation.paste: Paste subtitle text (SRT, VTT, or ASS)
